subject: Geography
title: Canyons and Gorges
description: A quick, practical guide to Canyons and Gorges.

hook.text: The Grand Canyon is 1.6 km deep, carved by the Colorado River over millions of years.

buildup.text: Water dissolves rock slowly, but given enough time even a creek can cut a canyon.

discovery.text: Tibet's Yarlung Tsangpo Canyon is actually deeper than the Grand Canyon.

twist.text: Slot canyons can flash-flood with no warning—dry one moment, deadly the next.

climax.text: Every canyon is a geological timeline. Walk down and you walk back through eras.

quiz.question: What canyon is deeper than the Grand Canyon?
